vulnerability.reason: Both describe a condition where hyperparameter tuning without empirical validation creates a gap between claimed and actual privacy leakage.
vulnerability.reference: Tuning hyperparameters without empirical privacy testing creates a gap between claimed epsilon-delta and actual leakage.
vulnerability.generated: Data subjects rely on theoretical DP guarantees without visibility into hyperparameter choices that cause actual leakage to exceed those bounds.

impact.reason: Both state that personal details from training text are memorized and exposed, with affected party and damage type matching.
impact.reference: Fine-tuned LLMs memorize and regurgitate personal details from training text.
impact.generated: Private text is memorized and extractable beyond the promised privacy level, exposing sensitive personal details and enabling re-identification.

dose_response.reason: Causal arc includes miscalibration and memorization, but the reference ends with reputational harm while the generated ends with re-identification, missing that final harm link.
dose_response.reference: DP hyperparameter tuning causes miscalibration, enabling fine-tuned LLMs to memorize personal text, exposing data subjects to reputational harm.
dose_response.generated: Empirically variable privacy guarantees causing miscalibration between theoretical DP bounds and actual leakage, through exposure of fine-tuning data subjects, trigger reliance on theoretical guarantees without hyperparameter visibility, leading to extractable private text and re-identification.
